name: Rosie's Award-Winning Brownies
recipeIngredient: 8 tablespoons Unsalted butter, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 325°F. Lightly grease an 8-inch square pan with butter or vegetable oil.
Melt the chocolate and butter in the top of a double boiler placed over simmering water. Allow cooling for 5 minutes. Pour the chocolate mixture into a medium-size mixing bowl containing the sugar. Mix until well combined, about 25 seconds. Scrape the bowl using a rubber spatula.
Add the vanilla extract. Using an electric mixer on low speed, add each egg one at a time, blending after each addition until thoroughly incorporated, approximately 10 seconds per egg. Scrape the bowl once more and mix until smooth and velvety for about 15 additional seconds.
Gradually fold in the flour using low speed, continuing to mix for an additional 20 seconds. Complete any remaining mixing by hand to ensure all ingredients are evenly distributed, including any flour at the bottom of the bowl. Stir in half of the chopped walnuts.
Evenly spread the batter into the prepared pan and sprinkle the remaining chopped walnuts on top.
Bake the brownies on the center oven rack until a thin crust forms on top and a tester inserted in the center comes out with moist crumbs, approximately 35 minutes. The center should not rise as much as the edges but will remain moist. Allow cooling for one hour before cutting; serving the next day enhances the flavor development.
Makes 9-12 brownies.
